title: Pillars of Creation (Eagle Nebula, M16)
explanation: Towering columns of cold interstellar gas and dust in the Eagle Nebula, about 6,500 light-years away, sculpted by radiation from nearby young, hot stars. Inside these pillars, new stars are being born. This is bundled demo data — add a free NASA_API_KEY to .env.local to see today's real Astronomy Picture of the Day.
